# Software Factory for VS Code 2.7

Release 2.7 is a workflow-governance and validation-infrastructure maturity
release built on the 2.6 production-readiness baseline. The factory now ships
a fully classifier-driven CI system with a four-level local mirror wrapper, an
explicit formatter-first repair ladder enforced as a canonical guardrail,
session-ownership contracts that isolate concurrent agent sessions, hardened
autonomous-delegation rules restricting the harness-bypass agent to human-only
activation, and a mature wiki-workflow surface with three routing lanes.
Ninety-three commits over thirty-plus addressed issues close this cycle.

## Highlights

- **Formatter-first is now the official canonical repair gate** — the narrow
  deterministic repair sequence (touched-file formatter → single failing test
  → touched bundle → broader validation) is named, documented in guardrails,
  locked in regression tests, and enforced across all workflow skills.
- **Four-level local CI mirror** — `local_ci_parity.py` now maps exactly to the
  GitHub CI hierarchy (`pr`, `focused-local`, `merge`, `production`) with
  watchdog-bounded bundles, structured output semantics, and explicit local/CI
  parity narration.
- **Session-unique execution surface ownership** — concurrent agent sessions can
  no longer silently steal each other's execution surface; every session must
  carry a matching `execution_lease_id` in the queue checkpoint.
- **Harness-bypass agent restricted to human-only activation** — standard agents
  are now explicitly prohibited from invoking the bypass agent autonomously,
  with regression-locked enforcement text.
- **Classifier-driven CI with concurrency control and caching** — `ci.yml`
  routes all validation through official bundle jobs, cancels superseded runs,
  and caches dependencies so repeated runs are substantially faster.

## Included enhancements

### Validation & CI infrastructure

- Four-level local mirror wrapper for `local_ci_parity.py` (issues #238, #239)
- Classifier-driven CI bundle jobs with concurrency cancellation and dependency caching (issues #277, #316, #317, #318)
- Canonical validation policy schema, official bundle taxonomy, and policy contract lock tests (issues #226–#228, #283, #285)
- Shared validation runner contract and compatibility adapters (issues #234–#236)
- Non-authoritative agent surfaces consuming validation policy (issue #321)
- Lock mirrored validation semantics in regression tests (issue #320)
- ADR-006 and operator docs updated for mirrored validation (issue #319)
- Bounded PR-check waiting and watchdog-bounded local CI parity bundles (issues #232, #276)
- Enriched shared runner watchdog diagnostics (issues #231, #286)
- Validation parity surfaces inventory (issue #224)
- Baseline validation runtimes and hotspot identification (issue #223)
- Fast-fail pytest CI parity lanes (issues #279, #280)
- CI parity speed improvements: reduce duplicated work (issue #277)
- Clean-main Black drift repair (issues #281, #282)

### Workflow guardrails & governance

- Formatter-first as explicit canonical narrow repair gate (issues #342, #345, #346)
- Session-unique execution surface ownership via `execution_lease_id` (issue #344)
- Harness-bypass agent manual-only restriction and regression lock (issues #338, #339)
- Autonomous delegation guardrail phase 1 — explicit escape hatch (issues #322, #334)
- Autonomous delegation guardrail phase 2 — terminal command restriction (issues #323, #335)
- Autonomous delegation guardrail phase 3 — validation output as state input (issues #324, #336)
- Autonomous delegation guardrail phase 4 — state consistency contracts (issues #325, #337)
- Workflow authority hardening: salvaged lost commits (issue #312)
- Require exact failure evidence before workflow repair retries (issues #303, #308)
- Require symbol grounding before issue-resolution edits (issues #302, #307)
- Require fresh GitHub truth before readiness claims (issues #301, #306)
- Shared validation plan resolver entrypoint and routing documentation (issue #313)
- Canonicalize fast evidence-first PR error resolution (issue #311)
- Canonical PR error resolve tactic prompt added (issue #311)
- execute-approved-plan routing hardened (issues #305, #310)
- resolve-issue wrapper explicitly hands off to @pr-merge (issue #315)

### Agent behavior & VS Code tasks

- VS Code task optimizations: targeted parity and auto-format tasks (issues #327, #331)
- Agent behavior restrictions (issues #328, #332)
- Script engine smart defaults (issues #329, #333)

### Wiki workflow

- Wiki bootstrap workflow (issues #252, #255)
- Three-lane wiki routing (bootstrap, authoring, maintenance) (issues #253, #256)
- Wiki bootstrap lane regression coverage (issues #254, #257)
- @wiki router guidance improvements (issues #259, #263)
- Reusable wiki skill boundary clarification (issues #260, #264)
- Wiki maintainer handoffs tightened (issues #261, #265)
- Wiki regression coverage for routing (issues #262, #266)
- Wiki naming and Home re-entry loop fix (issues #269, #270)
- Formatting-only docs README and wiki manifest diff captured (issues #249, #250)

### Bug fixes

- Work-issue quota overrides not reaching agent-worker — fixed (issue #314)
- Watchdog metadata contract made explicit (issues #230, #286)
- execute-approved-plan stray routing paths tightened (issues #305, #310)
- Resolve-issue missing @pr-merge handoff (issue #315)
- Clean-main formatter baseline drift repaired (issues #281, #282)

## Validation

Verified with:

- `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/local_ci_parity.py --level focused-local --base-rev main` → passed
- `./.venv/bin/python ./scripts/local_ci_parity.py --level merge --base-rev main` → passed
- `./.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` → all passing
- Post-release surface consistency: `VERSION`, `README.md` `## Current Release`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `.github/releases/v2.7.md`, and `manifests/release-manifest.json` all agree on `2.7`.

## Delivery status snapshot

| Scope                                    | Status    | Why it matters                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ---------------------------------------- | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Practical per-workspace baseline         | Improved  | Operators now have a validated formatter-first repair ladder, session-ownership contracts, and a four-level local CI mirror that matches GitHub CI exactly.                            |
| Workflow governance and agent discipline | Hardened  | Concurrent session isolation, harness-bypass restrictions, and four autonomous-delegation phases close the most significant workflow-safety gaps from the 2.6 baseline.                |
| Validation infrastructure maturity       | Advanced  | A classifier-driven CI system, shared validation runner, canonical policy schema, and official bundle taxonomy give the repository a stable, diagnosable validation backbone.          |
| Wiki workflow surface                    | Fulfilled | Three routing lanes, regression coverage, and maintainer handoffs give the wiki a complete supported operator story.                                                                   |
| Whole implementation roadmap             | Narrowed  | Workflow governance, validation infrastructure, and wiki surface all advanced significantly; remaining work focuses on runtime operational polish and further agent capability growth. |

## Notes for publishing

- Suggested tag: `v2.7`
- Canonical release marker: `VERSION`
- Machine-readable release source of truth: `manifests/release-manifest.json`
- Detailed release history: `CHANGELOG.md`
